benchmark_details.name: MoCa (Measuring Human-Language Model Alignment on Causal and Moral Judgment Tasks)
benchmark_details.overview: We collected a dataset of causal and moral judgment tasks from 24 cognitive science papers and developed a system to annotate each story with the factors they investigated. Using this dataset, we test whether large language models (LLMs) make causal and moral judgments about text-based scenarios that align with those of human participants.

purpose_and_intended_users.goal: To evaluate the alignment of large language models with human judgments regarding causal and moral scenarios, utilizing insights from cognitive science.
purpose_and_intended_users.limitations: The dataset focuses on specific aspects of alignment and may not generalize to broader contexts of moral reasoning.

data.source: Collected and annotated stories from 24 cognitive science papers.
data.annotation: Annotated by experts with the factors investigated in each story.

methodology.calculation: AMCE is computed to reveal implicit tendencies for each factor from human and LLM judgments.
methodology.interpretation: Higher alignment indicates better agreement between model outputs and human judgments.

targeted_risks.demographic_analysis: The dataset includes demographic variation in human responses but does not analyze demographic bias directly.

ethical_and_legal_considerations.consent_procedures: Participants gave consent through a notification form at the beginning of each study.
ethical_and_legal_considerations.compliance_with_regulations: IRB approval obtained for the data collection process.
